stem: A GPS navigation navigation system uses 4 parallel satellite links for redundancy. The position fixed requires at least one satellite link to function. satellite link 1 has a reliability of 83%. satellite link 2 has a reliability of 82%. satellite link 3 has a reliability between 69% and 79% (uncertain due to limited operational data). satellite link 4 has a reliability of 86%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all satellite links simultaneously, estimated between 2.0% and 6.3%. What is the overall probability that the position fixed?

ground_truth.answer: P(position fixed) ranges from 0.9358 to 0.9791. Point estimate: 0.9569.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 4 satellite links fail simultaneously).
With common-cause failure (2.0%-6.3%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9358, 0.9791].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
